note: Adding get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class, and refactoring image_to_base64 model_validator to use it instead of inline filetype.guess() logic

type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I'll own this file since both our changes touch image_to_base64/ImageBlock. Please send a DEFER+SPEC for normalize_image_bytes() so I can implement it alongside get_image_mimetype().

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Both features touch image_to_base64 in the same file/method. I'll own this file and implement both normalize_image_bytes() (mine) and get_image_mimetype() (yours) together in the refactored image_to_base64. Please DEFER with a SPEC for get_image_mimetype().

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a new method get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None on ImageBlock. Behavior: if self.image_mimetype is already set, return it immediately (do not re-guess). Otherwise, compute the mimetype by guessing from self.image bytes using the same logic currently inline in image_to_base64 (filetype.guess(self.image); use guess.mime if guess else None), cache the computed value into self.image_mimetype (so subsequent calls are O(1) and reuse the cached value), and return it. Note: image_to_base64's model_validator base64-encodes self.image in place, so if get_image_mimetype() is called after image_to_base64 has already run, self.image will be base64-encoded bytes rather than raw bytes -- that's fine, just reuse whatever guessing logic operates correctly given the current state of self.image (mirror existing behavior exactly, just factored into a callable method). Then update image_to_base64 (or your normalize_image_bytes refactor) to call self.get_image_mimetype() instead of inlining the filetype.guess(...) logic, so there is a single source of truth for mimetype resolution. No changes needed to resolve_image or other methods.
note: get_image_mimetype

type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add module-level helper (near top of types.py, after imports) with signature: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]. Behavior: it must detect whether the incoming 'image' bytes are already base64-encoded, and return a 2-tuple (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where encoded_image is the base64-encoded bytes ready to store on ImageBlock.image, and decoded_for_guessing is raw (non-base64) bytes suitable for passing to filetype.guess() for mimetype detection. Suggested implementation: try base64.b64decode(image, validate=True) inside a try/except; on success the input was already base64-encoded, so return (image, decoded) using the decoded bytes for guessing; on failure/exception, the input is raw bytes, so return (base64.b64encode(image), image) using the original raw bytes for guessing. Then refactor the existing image_to_base64 model_validator (currently: guesses mimetype via filetype.guess(self.image) BEFORE encoding, then unconditionally does self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)) to instead: if self.image is falsy return self early (unchanged); otherwise call encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype, set self.image_mimetype = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) mime (or None); finally set self.image = encoded_image; return self. This preserves current external behavior (mimetype guessing + final base64-encoded self.image) while adding correct handling for the case where self.image passed in is already base64-encoded (previously the code would double-encode it and guess the mimetype against base64 bytes instead of raw bytes). Feel free to compose this with your get_image_mimetype() as long as: (1) normalize_image_bytes is a standalone, independently callable/importable function (not a method) with exactly this name and this tuple return order (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing), and (2) image_to_base64 ends up calling it so self.image is always base64-encoded bytes after validation, using decoded_for_guessing (not self.image) for mimetype guessing.
note: Conceding file ownership to you since we both CLAIMed simultaneously - you own types.py, please implement both features there.

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None on ImageBlock: return self.image_mimetype if already set; else guess via filetype.guess(self.image), cache into self.image_mimetype, return it.
note: Acknowledged - I own types.py. Will implement normalize_image_bytes() (module-level helper) plus your get_image_mimetype() method (with caching into self.image_mimetype), and wire both into image_to_base64. Will send DONE when finished.

note: owning types.py, implementing both normalize_image_bytes and get_image_mimetype

In my copy: implemented normalize_image_bytes() (module-level helper, same signature/semantics as your spec) and get_image_mimetype() (caches into image_mimetype, lazily callable, correctly handles both raw and pre-base64-encoded self.image), wired into image_to_base64.
